2606.04990.title: From Agent Traces to Trust: Evidence Tracing and Execution Provenance in LLM Agents
2606.04990.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM)-based agents increasingly solve complex tasks by interacting with external tools, retrieval systems, memory modules, environments, and other agents. These capabilities expand agent autonomy, but also make agent behavior harder to verify, debug, and audit. Final-answer accuracy alone cannot explain how an output was produced, which evidence supported each claim, whether tool calls were justified, how memory influenced later decisions, or where execution failures originated. Evidence tracing and execution provenance address this gap by modeling how retrieved evidence, tool outputs, memory items, environment observations, intermediate claims, actions, and final answers are connected throughout agent execution. This survey provides a systematic review and conceptual framework for evidence tracing and execution provenance in LLM agents. We organize related work around a unified provenance perspective that connects retrieval grounding, claim support, tool-use safety, memory lineage, observability, debugging, audit, and recovery. We introduce a taxonomy covering trace sources, evidence and execution units, provenance relations, tracing granularity and timing, representation forms, and trust functions. We review key methodological directions, including provenance representation, evidence attribution, tool-use provenance, runtime guardrails, provenance-bearing memory, trace-based observability, and failure diagnosis. We also map existing benchmarks, datasets, and evaluation metrics to provenance-related capabilities, and discuss how evaluation can move from final-answer correctness toward process-level accountability. Finally, we outline open challenges, including unified trace schemas, claim-level and semantic provenance, provenance-aware safety mechanisms, realistic execution-trace benchmarks, recovery-oriented evaluation, and privacy-aware audit infrastructure.
2606.04990.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）基礎的代理人越來越多地通過與外部工具、檢索系統、記憶模塊、環境和其他代理人的互動來解決複雜任務。這些能力擴展了代理人的自主性，但也使得代理人的行為更難以驗證、除錯和審計。僅僅依賴最終答案的準確性無法解釋輸出是如何產生的，哪些證據支持了每一個主張，工具調用是否合理，記憶如何影響後續決策，或執行失敗的根源在哪裡。證據追蹤和執行來源通過建模檢索到的證據、工具輸出、記憶項目、環境觀察、中間主張、行動和最終答案在代理人執行過程中的連結來填補這一空白。本調查提供了對LLM代理人中證據追蹤和執行來源的系統性回顧和概念框架。我們圍繞統一的來源觀點組織相關工作，該觀點連接了檢索基礎、主張支持、工具使用安全性、記憶來源、可觀察性、除錯、審計和恢復。我們介紹了一個涵蓋追蹤來源、證據和執行單元、來源關係、追蹤粒度和時機、表示形式以及信任功能的分類法。我們回顧了關鍵的方法論方向，包括來源表示、證據歸屬、工具使用來源、運行時防護、承載來源的記憶、基於追蹤的可觀察性和故障診斷。我們還將現有的基準、數據集和評估指標映射到與來源相關的能力，並討論如何將評估從最終答案的正確性轉向過程層級的問責制。最後，我們概述了開放挑戰，包括統一的追蹤架構、主張層級和語義來源、來源感知的安全機制、現實的執行追蹤基準、以恢復為導向的評估以及隱私感知的審計基礎設施。
2606.04990.author: Yiqi Wang et.al.
2606.04990.authors: Yiqi Wang, Jiaqi Zhang, Taotao Cai, Zirui Liu, Qingqiang Sun, Zequn Sun, Zhangkai Wu, Mingkai Zhang, Yanming Zhu
